titleStmt.title: Letter from John F. Smyth to Robert Chalmers, 13 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John F. Smyth

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from a Monaghan based solicitor John F. Smyth to the Under Secretary Sir Robert Chalmers (1858–1938). After the Easter rising of 1916 Chalmers was sent to Ireland as under-secretary, replacing Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell (1850-1933), his chief secretary. Smyth is writhing on behalf of Miss Kate Rooney, and is enclosing a petition from Miss Rooney for a Temporary License for her premises in Castleblayney, Monaghan.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/8165

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Castleblayney,
Co. Monaghan . 13th May, 1916.
James Rooney Deceased. Sir, I beg to enclose Petition of Miss Kate Rooney for a Temporary
Licence for her premises in Main Street, Castleblayney from 30th
May to 3rd October next. I shall feel obliged by the matter receiving early attention
so that the Licence may be received by the 30th inst, and so enable
Miss Rooney to continue to carry on the Licensed business as here—
tofore and keep the trade to the premises. I am Sir,
Your obedient Servant, John F. Smyth The Under Secretary,
The Castle,
Dublin.
James Rooney Deceased Petition of Kate Rooney John F. Smyth
Solicitor for Petitioner
3 St Andrew Street
Dublin and Castleblayney
To His Excellency the Lord
Lieutenant General and General
Governor of Ireland. The Humble Petition of Kate Rooney
of Main Street, Castleblayney in the
County of Monaghan, Publican and
Spinster showeth That James Rooney was the owner
of Licensed premises situate in Main
Street, in the town of Castleblayney
in the parish of Muckno, Barony of
Cremorne, and County of Monaghan. That the said James Rooney died
on the 19th day of March 1916, having
previously duly executed his Will
whereby he bequeathed the said premises
to his sister your Petitioner subject
to the payment of his debts funeral
and testamentary expenses in said
Will mentioned. That the Executors named in said
Will have taken proceedings to prove
the said Will. That your Petitioner at Petty
Sessions held at Castleblayney on
the 4th April 1916 obtained a Pro-
visional transfer of the said Licence
until the Quarter Sessions for the
District after the expiration of one
month from that date, which Quarter
Sessions will be held on the 30th
May 1916. That the Licence Duty £12:5:3
has been paid to the Collector of
Customs and Excise Dundalk
in respect of the said premises up to the
30th September 1916. That by an oversight Notice of
Application for a transfer of said
Licence from said James Rooney
deceased to me was not served twenty
one days prior to the Quarter Sessions
to be held on the 30th inst, and in
consequence thereof your Petitioner
will be unable to obtain such transfer
at the said Quarter Sessions. That your Petitioner will apply
at the next Quarter Sessions to be
held in October next for a transfer
of said Licence. That your Petitioner has resided
with her brother the said James
Rooney deceased all her lifetime and
has for upwards of thirty years
assisted him in the management
and carrying on of said business
which has been established for
upwards of Fifty years. That the said business is your
Petitioners and only means of livelihood
and it is for the benefit of your
Petitioner that the said premises
should be kept open from the
Quarter Sessions on the 30th May until
the next Quarter Sessions on the 3rd
October 1916, so that the trade there-
to may not be diverted from the
premises. Your Petitioner therefore humbly
prays that it may please your
Excellency to grant to your Petitioner
a temporary Licence for the said
premises from 30th May until the
3rd October 1916 the date of the
next Quarter Sessions for the District.
And your Petitioner will ever pray Kate Rooney
